name: Garvin Bushell
abstract: Garvin Bushell (né Garvin Lamont Payne; September 25, 1902 – October 31, 1991) was an American clarinetist, saxophonist, and bassoonist. He performed with many prominent 20th-century jazz musicians, including Jelly Roll Morton and John Coltrane.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet

quotes.text: Carnegie Hall. We recorded it in Carnegie Hall and we went into Carnegie Hall at 12:00 o'clock -- oh, (Well upon) Wilman Braud was the base player, not Pop Foster, Broe. William Braud, that's the name -- he's from New Orleans. And we went in at 12:00 o'clock and it just began to snow. When we came out at 5:00 that evening the busses were straddling the roads. There was snow, I think, three feet deep. That's how hard it had snowed in New York while we were recording. I remember Bunk saying -- he'd never seen snow, you know. He had never seen snow.
quotes.caption: Garvin Bushell recalls recording with Bunk Johnson in heavy snow

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: You had a boy named Slick Jones... Hank Jones... Bobby Starks... And Fat Man Turner on bass, Eddie Mallory on the trumpet, and Fred -- Freddy Robinson. And a boy named Garvin Bushell... I stayed with them a long time

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: But like I tell you, man, Buster Bailey, Bushell -- you remember Garvin Bushell? ... MR. GREER: He played all the lead instruments.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: I knew all the guys in the band, because I worked with them before. ... Doc and his band, Garvin Bushell was on ... the sax

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...instrumentalists like Bennie Carter and Chick Webb and Don Redmon and Garvin Bushell and Sam Wooding...

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: Him and this fellow Joe Muranyi and Bob Wilber... They’re old enough but they went and saw Garvin Bushell
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: Garvin Bushell had just left. Bushell had got fired, and they got Jeff a week before I joined the band.
